summary: Backend-only regression for Iter57 polish retry inbox + transient-Mongo guard. Wrote /app/backend/tests/test_polish_retry_inbox.py with 16 tests across 7 classes covering: (a) GET /polish/failed — auth gate, response shape (book_id/title/author/fandom/filename/polish_attempts/polish_last_error/polish_failed_at, _id stripped), desc sort by polish_failed_at, user isolation, Trash exclusion, empty-inbox shape; (b) POST /polish — resets polish-failed→pending AND $unsets polish_attempts/polish_last_error/polish_failed_at (verified directly in Mongo); pending books untouched; (c) GET /polish/stats — still returns {pending,failed,arriving,in_progress} with int+bool types and correct counts; (d) Queue-summary integration — polish_failed count matches /polish/failed.count == /polish/stats.failed; (e) POST /polish/{book_id} — still 404s on unknown id; (f) _polish_drain transient vs permanent — AutoReconnect leaves book classifier='pending' with no sentinel fields, ValueError marks polish-failed with attempts=1, polish_last_error containing the exception text, polish_failed_at as parseable ISO; (g) is_transient_mongo_error predicate sanity. All 16 PASS in 5.97s. Cross-suite regression: pre-existing test_upload_queue_summary.py still 9/9 PASS.
critical_code_review_comments: polish_worker._polish_drain (utils/polish_worker.py L163-233) correctly routes AutoReconnect/NetworkTimeout/ServerSelectionTimeoutError/NotPrimaryError/ConnectionFailure/WaitQueueTimeoutError through is_transient_mongo_error → log+return (book stays pending). Permanent path increments polish_attempts off the previous value, captures str(exc)[:200] as polish_last_error, and writes polish_failed_at as ISO — all confirmed end-to-end against Mongo.

Sentinel write itself is wrapped in retry_on_transient with label='polish-sentinel-write' (L213-224), so a blip mid-sentinel doesn't lose the audit trail. The inner-most except (L225) just logs — fine, the recovery cron re-picks pending books anyway.

POST /polish (routes/polish.py L69-93) update_many filter is correctly scoped to user_id + classifier=polish-failed + category≠Trash. The $set/$unset combo is atomic and the $unset uses empty-string sentinel values per Mongo convention. Verified: pending books are NOT inadvertently $unset'd because the filter excludes them.

GET /polish/failed (routes/polish.py L96-128) projects {_id:0} explicitly + only the 8 lightweight fields the UI needs — no full-book payload leakage. Sort+limit(200) caps payload at a sensible size. Auth-gated via get_current_user dep.

polish_attempts is incremented monotonically (b.get('polish_attempts') or 0)+1 — but the drain re-queries only classifier='pending' books each round, and a transient failure now leaves the book pending. So on the next drain iteration the SAME book will increment attempts again? No — transient path returns BEFORE touching polish_attempts, so the counter is only bumped on permanent failures. Good. (Worth keeping in mind if a transient failure ever falls into the permanent branch by mistake; current code is correct.)

Subtle concern: the transient branch returns silently from _one() but the OUTER `while True` re-queries pending books. If Mongo is genuinely down for >2 min, the drain could spin tightly (find returns batch, polish_one_book raises AutoReconnect, returns, re-query, repeat). Today there's no backoff/sleep between iterations. A real Atlas failover would still resolve in seconds, but a longer outage could cause CPU churn. Suggest a small `await asyncio.sleep(2)` if the entire batch raised transient — out of scope for this fix but worth a follow-up issue.

polish_worker._sem is module-level and lazily bound to the first event loop that calls _get_sem(). In tests this is fine because the session-scoped shared_event_loop is pinned by conftest. In production it binds to uvicorn's main loop on first use, which is also fine. Documented behavior.
test_credentials: Each test registers a fresh TEST_polish-<uuid>@example.com via /api/auth/register and authenticates via Authorization: Bearer <session_token> extracted from the Set-Cookie header (the cookie itself is Secure-flagged so requests drops it over http://localhost). Direct-async tests insert synthetic users + sessions directly into Mongo. No reliance on shelfsort-tester@example.com.
seed_data_creation: Each API test creates 1-2 ephemeral users and 1-3 polish-failed book rows (classifier='polish-failed', polish_attempts/polish_last_error/polish_failed_at preset). Direct-async tests insert one pending book per test. All rows TEST_-prefixed, all torn down in fixture cleanup. No persistent state.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run: `sudo supervisorctl restart backend && sleep 10 && cd /app/backend && python -m pytest tests/test_polish_retry_inbox.py -v` (6s). IMPORTANT: uvicorn watches /app/backend recursively including tests/, so editing the test file triggers a backend reload which hangs in-flight HTTP requests. Always restart backend before running fresh after edits. For the transient-vs-permanent drain tests, the test uses asyncio.wait_for(timeout=2.0) on the transient branch because that branch deliberately keeps the book pending → drain would otherwise loop forever; this is intentional, mirrors the real recovery-cron behavior. BASE URL defaults to https://genre-sort.preview.emergentagent.com via REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL.
rca of the issue: Original 'a few books wouldn't polish' bug RCA: utils/polish_worker.py:_polish_drain's exception handler was sentinelizing ALL exceptions as classifier='polish-failed' regardless of whether the exception was a transient Mongo blip (Atlas failover, AutoReconnect) or a genuine permanent error. With Atlas under load + occasional elections, a small percentage of books would hit a transient error mid-classify and get permanently flagged — stranding work that would have polished fine on retry. The fix (L194-200) calls is_transient_mongo_error(exc) on the typed predicate (utils/db_retry.py:50) and short-circuits to `return` (book stays pending) for AutoReconnect/ServerSelectionTimeoutError/NetworkTimeout/NotPrimaryError/ConnectionFailure/WaitQueueTimeoutError. The polish-recovery cron (every 5 min) then re-picks the book on the next tick. Verified via direct-async test against a real Motor client with monkeypatched polish_one_book raising AutoReconnect: book stays classifier='pending', no polish_attempts/polish_last_error/polish_failed_at set. Permanent ValueError test confirms the sentinel-write path still works correctly (polish_attempts=1, error captured, ISO timestamp set).
